title: T&C Engineer - Electrification
description:
T&C Engineer - Electrification Projects

Navsar Global is a premier specialist services firm, connecting top-tier talent with leading companies in critical infrastructure sectors such as power generation, data centres, and oil & gas. We are currently seeking a highly skilled and dedicated T&C Engineer to join our client's dynamic team, focusing on impactful Electrification Projects.

The Role:

As a T&C Engineer, you will be a pivotal member of the project team, coordinating daily testing and commissioning workflows on-site. You will take full ownership of all testing activities for complex Electrification Projects, ensuring meticulous adherence to defined standards, customer requirements, economic objectives, quality benchmarks, schedule timelines, and stringent Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) protocols.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate effectively with internal teams, various departments, clients, and system-wide contractors and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities seamlessly.
  • Actively participate in critical meetings such as Works Train Notice Meetings, Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings, and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e and HGBD works).
  • Prepare and submit necessary documentation, including SDAR (Site Daily Activity Report) and TAR (Track Access Request), to secure track access and ensure testing proceeds on schedule.
  • Lead and manage commissioning activities across the entire lifecycle, from Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Pre-Site Acceptance Testing (PRE-SAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), through to final handover to the client and successful service commencement.
  • Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, detailed step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork.
  • Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned.
  • Supervise and provide hands-on support to installation teams for SKD RT A&A (Installation activities) at the site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient working environment.
  • Assist the Manager in preparing comprehensive work schedule programs.
  • Undertake ad-hoc duties assigned by the Manager that are critical to the project's success.
  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative effort.
  • Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate.
  • Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments.
  • Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives.

Qualifications:

Education & Training:

  • High School Diploma or equivalent.
  • Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering.
  • Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred.

Experience:

  • Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects.
  • Proven track record of taking responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and outcomes.
  • Demonstrated experience in using requirements traceability.
  • Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage.
  • Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports.
  • Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works.
  • Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority.
  • Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR).
  • Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi).
  • Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems.

Technical Competencies:

  • Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market.
  • Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components.
  • Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication.
  • Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities.
  • Basic knowledge and application of virtual communication and collaboration tools.
  • Advanced understanding of internal and external laws and regulations to ensure ethically proper business acumen; ability to advise colleagues on compliance-related issues.
  • Basic knowledge of contract formats, practices, and policies, understanding when and whom to involve for assistance.
  • Basic understanding and application of information security practices to protect data and systems.
  • Basic knowledge of regional labor laws and regulations, applying them to daily work and knowing escalation procedures.
  • Basic knowledge of the relevant product, system, and service portfolio within the area of responsibility, including ability to suggest process improvements and solve simple technical problems.
  • Basic technical product and system knowledge relevant to the organization, understanding technical differences and utilizing documentation effectively.

postedCompany.description:
Navsar is a Singapore-based business and solutions provider for the Energy, Power, and Infrastructure industries.
Services: The company offers services such as engineering, procurement, and construction management (EPCm), system integration, technical staffing, and financed upgrades/retrofitting solutions.
postedCompany.name: NAVSAR ENGINEERING INTERNATIONAL PTE. LTD.
